source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / goal-directed reasoning literature
description: AROMER maintains an explicit goal representation at all times: what is the stated objective, what is the implied objective, and are they aligned?

components: safety_goal: must not conflict with REMORA policy
payload.failure_mode: Acting on stated_goal when implied_goal is harmful — classic jailbreak framing
payload.aromer_action: If stated_goal and implied_goal diverge by >0.3 cosine distance, raise to VERIFY

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / probabilistic reasoning
description: AROMER represents uncertainty explicitly as a probability interval, not as a binary known/unknown. Every belief carries a confidence bound.

contradicted.note: Conflicting high-confidence sources — must not act until resolved

payload.aromer_action: Threshold actions by confidence level. Unknown or contradicted → ABSTAIN or ESCALATE

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / risk-aware planning
description: Risk has three axes: probability of harm, magnitude of harm, and reversibility. AROMER must represent all three, not just tier labels.

payload.escalation_rule: If reversibility=irreversible AND magnitude>=high → always ESCALATE regardless of p_harm
payload.aromer_action: Compute composite risk score = p_harm * magnitude_weight * (2 if irreversible else 1)

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / POMDP / Bayesian inference
description: AROMER maintains a belief state over the current context: what is known, what is observed, and what must be inferred. Beliefs are updated as evidence arrives.

state_components: context_type: what kind of request is this?
actor_trust: how much to trust the requester?
scope: local | team | org | public | internet
prior_episodes: similar past cases and their outcomes
payload.update_rule: Bayesian update on new evidence. Do not anchor to initial interpretation.
payload.aromer_action: Re-evaluate belief state at each reasoning step. Surface high-uncertainty components.

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / selective attention
description: Not all features of a request are equally important. AROMER allocates attention to high-salience features: scope markers, irreversibility signals, and safety-relevant terms.

high_salience_features: scope_words: all, every, entire, global, production, everyone
irreversibility_words: delete, drop, destroy, purge, terminate, overwrite
escalation_words: critical, emergency, urgent, override, bypass, force
payload.salience_rule: If >=2 high_salience_features detected, downgrade trust_score by 0.15
payload.aromer_action: Log attention features in decision trace for auditability

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / deliberative planning
description: Before committing to an action, AROMER generates candidate next steps, evaluates each against safety invariants, and selects the one with minimum risk compatible with the goal.

payload.pruning_rule: Discard any candidate that triggers a safety invariant
payload.selection_rule: Choose highest ranked. If all pruned → ABSTAIN
payload.aromer_action: Surface candidate reasoning in decision trace when verdict is non-ACCEPT
